fuzzy_prd: Hey team, we need to wrap up the markdown rendering library integration. The core engine is mostly there but we need a proper adapter that reads JSON from stdin and writes results to stdout — basically the same pattern we used on that content pipeline project last quarter, you know the one. The adapter needs to handle all the rendering modes we've talked about: the default HTML output, that normalized re-serialization mode, and the XML tree dump. Extensions should be toggleable per-request too — things like tables, checkboxes on list items, the strikethrough/superscript stuff, auto-detected links, footnotes, that anchor-on-headings thing, and definition lists. There's also some safety stuff around raw HTML passthrough that the security team flagged — by default it should be suppressed, but callers can opt in. Oh and there's a tag filtering layer on top of that when unsafe mode is on. Smart punctuation (the curly quotes / em-dash thing) lives on the parse side not the render side, just FYI. Bullet marker style and line-wrap width are configurable for the normalized output mode. If anything goes wrong parsing the request or running the render, we should still return a well-formed JSON error rather than crashing. The whole thing needs to stay decoupled — the business logic shouldn't know anything about stdin/stdout.

trigger_keywords: default format
what format if format field is omitted
output mode when format is not specified
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When the `format` field is absent from the JSON input, the output format defaults to HTML rendering. Only the explicit values `"commonmark"` and `"xml"` switch to those respective modes.

trigger_keywords: unsafe html default
raw html blocked by default
when is raw HTML allowed through
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Raw HTML blocks and inline HTML are suppressed by default. A caller must explicitly set `render.unsafe_html` to `true` in the JSON input for raw HTML to pass through to the output.

trigger_keywords: tag filter
which tags are blocked
dangerous tag filtering
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Tag filtering is a separate opt-in controlled by `render.tag_filter`. When enabled alongside `render.unsafe_html: true`, it escapes a specific set of dangerous tag names (script, plaintext, iframe, textarea, style, xmp, title, noembed, noframes, noscript, listing) even though unsafe HTML is otherwise allowed.

trigger_keywords: smart punctuation location
where does smart quotes config go
smart punctuation parse or render
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Smart punctuation (curly quotes, apostrophes, em dashes, en dashes, ellipses) is configured under the `parse` key, not the `render` key. The correct input field is `parse.smart_punctuation: true`.

trigger_keywords: list marker style
bullet marker config
commonmark dash or asterisk
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The bullet list marker style for CommonMark output is configured as `render.list_marker`, accepting values such as `"dash"`. This only affects output when `format` is `"commonmark"`.

trigger_keywords: heading anchor prefix
anchor id prefix
user-content prefix for heading ids
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When the `header_id_anchors` extension is enabled, each heading gets a prepended anchor element. The `id` attribute uses the prefix `user-content-` (e.g., `id="user-content-hi"`), the `href` fragment uses the bare slug (e.g., `href="#hi"`), and the anchor has `aria-hidden="true"` and `class="anchor"` with empty content. Repeated slugs get numeric suffixes starting at `-1` for the second occurrence of a given base slug, but note that counter increments are per base-slug across all headings globally.

trigger_keywords: task list checkbox html
checkbox input attributes
disabled checked checkbox markup
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Task list checkboxes render as `<input type="checkbox" disabled="" />` for unchecked items and `<input type="checkbox" checked="" disabled="" />` for checked items. The `unsafe_html` render option must also be `true` for these input elements to appear in output; without it they are suppressed.

trigger_keywords: footnote backref attributes
footnote section markup
footnote id format
hidden_constraints.oracle_response:
Footnote references render as `{n}`. The trailing footnotes section is `
` containing an `
    `. Each definition item is `
  1. ` and the backref anchor has `class="footnote-backref"`, `data-footnote-backref`, `data-footnote-backref-idx="{n}"`, and `aria-label="Back to reference {n}"`.

trigger_keywords: strikethrough single or double tilde
del element tilde syntax
single tilde vs double tilde
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: With the `strikethrough` extension enabled, both single-tilde (`~text~`) and double-tilde (`~~text~~`) spans render as `<del>` elements. Surrounding punctuation that is not part of the span (e.g., a trailing `?` or `.`) remains outside the `<del>` tag.

trigger_keywords: autolink email format
autolink www prefix
bare url scheme detection
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: With the `autolink` extension: bare `www.` addresses become `<a href="http://www.{rest}">` links; bare email addresses become `<a href="mailto:{email}">` links; full scheme URLs (e.g., `https://`) link to themselves. Text that only resembles an address but is malformed remains plain text.

trigger_keywords: xml format output structure
commonmark xml doctype
xml source positions
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When `format` is `"xml"`, output begins with `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>` and `<!DOCTYPE document SYSTEM "CommonMark.dtd">`, then a `<document xmlns="http://commonmark.org/xml/1.0">` root. Nodes are indented with 2 spaces. Source position attributes are optional and only included when explicitly requested via `render.sourcepos: true`.

trigger_keywords: error response format
invalid json input handling
what happens on bad input
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: If the JSON input is malformed or a required field is missing or invalid, the adapter must return a well-formed JSON error object rather than panicking or writing partial output. The error should be surfaced as a structured response, not a raw process crash.

trigger_keywords: description list syntax
dl dt dd markup
term and definition colon prefix
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: With the `description_lists` extension, terms followed by colon-prefixed definition lines render as `<dl>` containing `<dt>` and `<dd>` elements. The opening `<dl>` tag and first `<dt>` share the same line in output (no newline between them). Inline markup inside terms and definitions is parsed normally.

trigger_keywords: table alignment attribute
th td align center left right
delimiter row alignment markers
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Table cells without alignment markers emit no `align` attribute. Cells under a `:---:` (center), `:---` (left), or `---:` (right) delimiter receive `align="center"`, `align="left"`, or `align="right"` respectively. Header cells (`<th>`) and body cells (`<td>`) both receive the alignment attribute.

trigger_keywords: how should I organize the code
single file or multiple files
project structure
directory layout
should I split into multiple files
monolithic or modular
how complex is the codebase
what files should I create
how to structure the implementation
should I separate business logic from IO
decouple core from IO
should business logic touch stdin stdout
how to separate parsing from logic
SOLID principles
separation of concerns
adapter pattern
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Scale-driven code organization: match physical structure to domain complexity. For micro-utilities or simple scripts a single well-organized file is fine. For systems with multiple distinct responsibilities (I/O routing, business rules, formatters, etc.) you MUST use a multi-file layout (e.g. src/, tests/) — never a single god file. Do not over-engineer simple problems, but strictly avoid monolithic files for complex domains. Strict separation of concerns is mandatory: core business logic must be completely decoupled from stdin/stdout and JSON parsing. The JSON test cases are a black-box contract for the execution adapter only — NOT the internal data model. Follow SOLID: SRP (separate parsing, routing, core execution, output formatting); OCP (core engine open for extension, closed for modification); DIP (high-level modules depend on abstractions, not I/O details). The public interface must be idiomatic. Errors must be modeled properly (typed exceptions or Result/Monad patterns), not generic faults.

trigger_keywords: how does the dispatcher work
how is the program invoked
stdin stdout interface
execution adapter
how to read input
how does the test harness call my code
what is the entry point for testing
how does the runner call the program
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: You must implement an execution adapter (dispatcher) that is logically and ideally physically separate from the core domain. It reads a single JSON object from stdin, inspects the 'action' field to route to the correct core function, then prints the result to stdout matching the per-feature output contract. All policy durations in inputs are in seconds; all time points are epoch milliseconds. The adapter must not contain any business logic — every numeric result and boolean decision is delegated to the core domain.

trigger_keywords: how to write test.sh
test harness scaffold
automated test script
how should test.sh work
cases-dir parameter
where does test output go
output file naming format
how are test outputs named
rcb_tests structure
test scaffold deliverable
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Deliver a bash test harness at rcb_tests/test.sh. It must accept --cases-dir <subdir> (default: test_cases) to point at a directory of *.json case files under rcb_tests/. For each case file it runs every case in the 'cases' array against the dispatcher, writing raw stdout (no PASS/FAIL metadata) to: rcb_tests/stdout/<cases-dir>/{filename_stem}@{case_index_zero_padded_3}.txt Example: rcb_tests/stdout/public_test_cases/feature1_next_retry_at@000.txt Output is namespaced by <cases-dir> so different directories never overwrite each other. A single entry point 'bash rcb_tests/test.sh' runs the full suite with the default dir.

hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Yes, public test cases are provided, one file per feature. Ask me for a specific feature by name and I will give you the full test case JSON.

context_pointers.vague_reference: the same pattern we used on that content pipeline project last quarter
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The JSON stdin/stdout execution adapter pattern described in the Architecture section: reading a JSON object from stdin with fields `markdown`, `format`, `extensions`, `parse`, and `render`, then writing a JSON object to stdout with an `output` field (or an error field on failure).

The `header_id_anchors` extension (Feature 8), which prepends each heading with an `<a>` anchor element using `user-content-` prefixed ids and bare-slug hrefs, with deduplication via numeric suffixes.

context_pointers.vague_reference: the security team flagged — by default it should be suppressed
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The `render.unsafe_html` flag (Feature 3): raw HTML blocks and inline HTML are stripped/escaped by default; they only pass through when `render.unsafe_html: true` is explicitly set in the request.

context_pointers.vague_reference: Smart punctuation (the curly quotes / em-dash thing) lives on the parse side not the render side
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Feature 13: the configuration key is `parse.smart_punctuation: true`, not `render.smart_punctuation`. It converts straight quotes, apostrophes, ellipses, en dashes, and em dashes to their typographic Unicode equivalents during the parse phase.

oracle_data.fallback_response: That detail isn't something I can answer off the top of my head — you'll want to check the PRD (start.md) and the feature test cases under rcb_tests/public_test_cases/ for the exact contract. The test JSON files are the ground truth for expected input/output shapes.

feature12_xml_output_format.json.description: Render parsed Markdown as CommonMark XML, optionally including source position attributes for document and child nodes.

feature13_smart_punctuation.json.description: When smart punctuation is enabled, replace straight quotes, apostrophes, ellipses, en dashes, and em dashes with typographic characters during HTML rendering.

feature1_core_block_rendering.json.description: Render ordinary block-level Markdown structures to HTML, including paragraphs, block quotes, headings, lists, thematic breaks, and fenced code blocks.

input.markdown:
My **document**.

It's mine.

> Yes.

## Hi!

Okay.

feature2_inline_text_and_links.json.description: Render inline Markdown syntax and character references to HTML while preserving escaped literals and encoding link targets.

feature3_raw_html_rendering.json.description: Render raw HTML according to safety options, allowing recognized raw blocks when unsafe output is enabled and filtering disallowed tag names when requested.

feature4_table_extension.json.description: When table parsing is enabled, convert pipe table syntax into HTML tables with header, body, alignment, and surrounding block context.

feature5_task_list_extension.json.description: When task-list parsing is enabled, convert list items beginning with checkbox markers into disabled checkbox inputs, preserving checked state and inline markup.

input.markdown:
- [ ] List item 1
- [ ] This list item is **bold**
- [x] There is some `code` here

cases.expected_output:
• List item 1
  • This list item is bold
  • There is some code here

feature6_inline_extension_markup.json.description: When optional inline extensions are enabled, render strikethrough and superscript delimiters as semantic HTML while leaving surrounding punctuation intact.

feature7_autolink_extension.json.description: When automatic link detection is enabled, convert bare web addresses and email addresses into links and leave malformed address-like text unchanged.

feature8_heading_anchor_extension.json.description: When heading anchors are enabled, prepend each heading with an anchor whose fragment is derived from normalized heading text and made unique for repeated headings.

input.markdown:
# Hi.
## Hi 1.
### Hi.
#### Hello.
##### Hi.
###### Hello.
# Isn't it grand?

cases.expected_output:
Hi.

Hi 1.

Hi.

Hello.

Hi.
Hello.

Isn't it grand?

feature9_footnote_extension.json.description: When footnote parsing is enabled, render valid references as numbered superscript links, append only referenced definitions, create backreferences, and leave invalid footnote syntax as ordinary text.
